_doc: BASELINE BENCHMARK: plain RAG vs graph recall. The bar the consciousness-graph is trying to beat. Crude keyword RAG used as the floor stand-in for pgvector (same retrieval SHAPE: top-k by relevance, no relational reasoning). Captured 2026-06-24.

query_classes.rag_note: RAG's HOME TURF — the answer is one contiguous passage (MBh 13.135). Crude keyword missed it (hit Agni Purana) but real pgvector semantic would nail it. Graph NOT needed here.
query_classes.verdict: TIE (RAG sufficient) — recitations live in chunks; don't need the graph

query_classes.rag_note: RAG fine for 'describe entity X' — fetch + summarize.
FAILS — no single chunk says it cleanly; keyword returned Agni Purana junk. Real pgvector would be hit-or-miss (the fact is implied across chunks).
query_classes.graph_note: Returns 'Surya --father--> Karna' as a literal edge.
query_classes.verdict: GRAPH WINS — the fact is a relationship, not a passage

query_classes.rag_note: FAILS HARD — returned Yoga Vasistha. The chain spans many chunks across the whole Awakener; no passage contains 'Babaji->Lahiri->Tinkori->Satyacharan->Sharma'.
query_classes.graph_note: Returns the full corrected chain as connected edges.
query_classes.verdict: GRAPH WINS DECISIVELY — RAG structurally cannot assemble a chain

query_classes.example: Is Babaji the being who met Shankaracharya
query_classes.rag_note: FAILS — returned Markandeya. The hypothesis links a figure in the Awakener to a Shankara legend; no single chunk co-states them.
query_classes.graph_note: Surfaces Babaji + Chandala + Shankaracharya together with the edge.
query_classes.verdict: GRAPH WINS DECISIVELY — cross-text identity is the graph's whole point

summary.the_bar: RAG handles 'fetch a passage' and 'describe one entity'. It FAILS on scattered facts, multi-hop chains, and cross-text identity — exactly the 3 classes the consciousness-graph exists to win. Future studies: beat RAG on SCATTERED/MULTI-HOP/CROSS-TEXT-ID; match it on PASSAGE/SINGLE-FACT.
